title: Moderated Activities
tagline: Bring analogue challenges into the digital journey.
short_description: A simple way to connect physical games and human-led activities with the BRDGE ecosystem. Each result, score, or snapshot is logged by a moderator through a tablet, keeping every guest's achievement part of the overall experience.
long_description: Not every experience needs a screen. This module bridges fully analogue activities – from can-throwing and hammer games to jump-height or strength challenges – with digital storytelling. A moderator equipped with a tablet records scores, uploads photos, or confirms participation, ensuring that even hands-on, physical games become part of the visitor's journey. The data flows seamlessly into the BRDGE system, enabling leaderboards, personal recaps, or digital souvenirs, just like any connected module.

items.title: Festivals, Fairs & Outdoor Events
items.description: Classic carnival games, strength challenges, or skill stations become fully connected — a moderator logs each result on the spot, feeding scores into a live leaderboard or personal recap visible across the event.

items.title: Brand Activations & Experiential Zones
items.description: Physical brand challenges — throwing, building, tasting, or testing — gain a digital layer without losing their tactile appeal. Moderators capture moments and scores that become part of the visitor's branded story.

items.title: Conferences, Team Events & Workshops
items.description: Group activities, team challenges, or workshop exercises that happen off-screen can still contribute to a collective journey — scores, results, and photos logged by a facilitator feed into the wider BRDGE experience.

items.title: Experience Extension
items.description: Expand the BRDGE journey beyond screens and sensors — any physical game or human-led activity becomes a connected touchpoint, ensuring no moment is lost from the visitor's story.

items.title: Gamified Motivation
items.description: Knowing their score is recorded and visible on a leaderboard raises the stakes of every physical challenge — visitors push harder, return for rematches, and engage more deeply with the activity.

items.title: Data Collection & Insights
items.description: Physical activities generate real behavioral and performance data — moderated logging turns analogue play into structured metrics that inform event reporting and visitor profiling.

items.title: Memorable Shareable Moments
items.description: A photo captured by a moderator at the peak of a challenge becomes a personal digital souvenir — shareable, branded, and tied to the visitor's unique token and journey recap.

items.title: Physical activities that fall outside the digital journey
items.description: Analogue games and human-led challenges are often invisible to the data layer. Moderated Activities bridges that gap — every physical result is captured and connected to the visitor's overall experience.

items.title: Inconsistent score tracking across stations
items.description: Without a system, moderators use clipboards, paper, or memory. This module gives them a standardised tablet interface — fast, consistent, and instantly synced to the backend.

items.title: Leaderboards that miss the most exciting moments
items.description: If the best performances happen at physical stations, they should appear on the leaderboard. Moderated logging ensures athletic or skill-based results compete alongside digital ones.

items.title: Visitors who disengage from screen-heavy experiences
items.description: Not everyone wants to stare at a device. Moderated Activities brings those visitors into the journey through play that feels natural — the digital layer works quietly in the background.

items.title: No visual memory of physical achievements
items.description: A great throw, a jump record, or a team challenge deserves a photograph. Moderators can capture and upload images in the moment — tied to the visitor's token and surfaced in their personal recap.

items.title: Fragmented multi-format events
items.description: Events that mix digital and physical activities often feel like two separate experiences. This module unifies them — physical scores, digital interactions, and media all flow into one coherent visitor record.

items.title: Proving engagement at unstaffed-feeling stations
items.description: A human moderator adds credibility, fairness, and energy to any challenge — and the digital log proves the interaction happened, with a timestamp and result to back it up.

items.title: Scaling physical activations without losing personalisation
items.description: At large events with many parallel stations, it's hard to maintain individual recognition. Moderated Activities ties every result back to a specific visitor — even across dozens of simultaneous challenges.

items.title: Moderator tablet interface
items.description: A clean, fast-entry screen for the activity facilitator — logging scores, confirming participation, adding notes, or uploading a photo with minimal friction during a live event.

items.title: Visitor identification flow
items.description: The moderator locates or scans the visitor's token (via QR, NFC, or manual lookup) to attach the result directly to their personal record in the BRDGE system.

items.title: Score and result entry logic
items.description: Configurable input fields for the specific activity — numeric score, time, distance, rank, or a simple participation confirmation — adapted to the challenge format.

items.title: Photo capture and upload
items.description: The moderator can take and upload a photo from the tablet in the moment — linked to the visitor's token and available in their post-visit gallery or digital souvenir.

items.title: Backend sync and analytics output
items.description: All logged results flow in real time into the BRDGE backend — feeding leaderboards, personal recaps, and the event's participation data export.

items.title: Physical activity equipment and props
items.description: The game itself — cans, hammers, targets, jump mats, or any physical prop — is entirely outside scope and must be sourced, built, and maintained by the client or event partner.

items.title: Moderator staffing
items.description: The human facilitators operating the tablet at each station are not provided. Staffing, briefing, and coordination of moderators is the client's responsibility.

items.title: Tablets or devices for moderators
items.description: The physical tablet hardware used by moderators is not included. A compatible device (iPad or Android tablet with camera and Wi-Fi) must be supplied per station.

items.title: Venue network infrastructure
items.description: Reliable Wi-Fi across all activity zones is required for real-time sync. Network provision, cabling, or temporary hotspot infrastructure is outside the module scope.

items.title: Custom physical game design or fabrication
items.description: Designing, building, or sourcing bespoke physical challenges or branded game props is a separate production or fabrication service, not part of this digital module.

items.trigger: The station is set up and the moderator's tablet is active — waiting for the next participant
items.experience: The tablet displays the station identity and a prompt to locate or scan the next visitor. The physical activity area is prepared and the moderator is in position.
items.feedback: Station name, activity title, and visitor lookup prompt on moderator screen
items.configurable: Station name, activity type label, branding on moderator interface

items.trigger: A visitor arrives at the activity station
items.experience: The moderator scans the visitor's QR code, taps their NFC token, or searches by name — pulling up their profile and linking the upcoming result to their record.
items.feedback: Visitor name and token confirmed on moderator screen; optional welcome or instruction displayed
items.configurable: Lookup method (scan, tap, manual search), confirmation message
items.data: Visitor token linked, arrival time at station

items.trigger: Visitor is identified and the physical activity begins
items.experience: The visitor performs the analogue challenge. The moderator observes, assists if needed, and prepares to log the result. A photo opportunity is flagged if configured.
items.feedback: Active state on moderator tablet — result entry fields ready, photo capture button available
items.configurable: Result input type (score, time, distance, rank, pass/fail), photo prompt toggle

items.trigger: The activity is complete — the moderator records the outcome
items.experience: The moderator enters the result and optionally uploads a photo. A single confirmation tap submits everything to the BRDGE backend — instantly updating the visitor's record, leaderboard, and recap.
items.feedback: Success confirmation on moderator tablet; optional real-time leaderboard update on nearby display
items.configurable: Result field labels, photo upload, confirmation message, leaderboard push toggle
items.data: Score or result logged, photo uploaded, leaderboard position updated, timestamp

items.trigger: Result is submitted and the visitor's journey record is updated
items.experience: The visitor can be shown their result, their leaderboard position, or a preview of their digital souvenir — before the station resets for the next participant.
items.feedback: Optional result display for the visitor (on tablet, nearby screen, or their own device), reset prompt for moderator
items.configurable: Visitor-facing result display, CTA (check leaderboard, view recap), reset flow
items.data: Confirmed result in visitor record, rank assigned, souvenir or recap triggered if configured

items.title: Activity type and result format
items.description: The input logic adapts to the challenge — a numeric score for distance or strength, a time for speed events, a simple pass/fail for participation confirmation, or a rank position in a head-to-head.

items.title: Station identity and branding
items.description: Each activity station can carry its own name, icon, and visual identity on the moderator interface — useful for multi-zone events with several simultaneous challenges.

items.title: Photo capture settings
items.description: Photo upload can be made mandatory, optional, or disabled entirely per station — and captured images can be auto-branded with overlays, frames, or event logos before delivery.

items.title: Visitor lookup method
items.description: Moderators can locate visitors by scanning a QR code, tapping an NFC token, or searching by name or entry number — adaptable to the physical token format in use.

items.title: Leaderboard display logic
items.description: Results from Moderated Activities can feed into a shared leaderboard alongside digital module scores — or maintain a separate physical challenge ranking, depending on the event structure.

items.title: Multilingual moderator interface
items.description: The tablet interface can be localised to the moderator's working language, independent of the visitor-facing language settings elsewhere in the system.

items.title: Visual identity
items.description: Colours, typography, and tone of the moderator interface and any visitor-facing output screens can be aligned to the overall brand or event theme.

items.title: Tablet per station
items.description: One compatible tablet (iPad or Android, with camera and Wi-Fi) is required per moderated activity station. Devices must be charged, connected, and logged into the moderator interface before the event opens.

items.title: Stable Wi-Fi across activity zones
items.description: Each station requires a reliable Wi-Fi connection for real-time result submission and photo upload. If activity zones are spread across a large or outdoor venue, network coverage must be confirmed in advance.

items.title: Moderator briefing and interface training
items.description: Each moderator needs a short briefing on the tablet flow — identifying visitors, entering results, capturing photos, and handling edge cases. A walkthrough guide or on-site training session is included in onboarding.

items.title: Visitor token system in place
items.description: Moderated Activities requires visitors to carry an active token (QR, NFC, or ID reference) that the moderator can scan or look up. The Visitor Token Pass module or equivalent must be operational.

items.title: Physical activity equipment (client-supplied)
items.description: All physical props, games, safety matting, signage, and any structural setup for the activity itself are the client's responsibility and must be installed and tested before go-live.

items.title: Station positioning and visitor flow
items.description: Each station should have clear queuing space, a defined activity area, and a comfortable position for the moderator — visible to participants but not obstructing the physical challenge.

items.title: Admin access for live monitoring
items.description: An event manager or operator should have access to the backend dashboard to monitor incoming results, flag anomalies, and export data in real time or post-event.

items.title: Participation log per station
items.description: A timestamped record of every visitor who took part in each physical activity — including their token ID, station, and result — forming a complete picture of physical engagement across the event.

items.title: Score and result records
items.description: Every logged result — numeric, time-based, rank, or pass/fail — is stored against the visitor's record and exportable for leaderboard management or post-event reporting.

items.title: Leaderboard contribution data
items.description: Physical activity scores feed directly into the overall event leaderboard — enabling a unified competitive picture that spans both analogue and digital interactions.

items.title: Photo archive linked to visitor tokens
items.description: All moderator-captured images are stored and associated with individual visitor records — accessible in personal recaps, post-visit galleries, or branded digital souvenir flows.

items.title: Station throughput and dwell time
items.description: How many visitors passed through each station, how long activities took on average, and peak traffic windows — useful for capacity planning and future event design.

items.title: Moderator activity log
items.description: A record of all submissions made per station and per operator — useful for quality control, shift management, and identifying any logging gaps or anomalies.

items.title: Exportable full results dataset
items.description: A structured export of all moderated activity data — visitor identities (anonymised if required), scores, photos, and timestamps — ready for reporting decks or CRM integration.

items.title: Visitor Token Pass
items.description: The essential companion — every moderated result is tied to a visitor's token, ensuring physical activity data flows into their personal journey record and post-visit recap.

items.title: Leaderboard Display Module
items.description: Physical activity scores can feed a live leaderboard displayed across the venue — making analogue performance visible to the full crowd and driving competitive motivation.

items.title: Journey Module
items.description: Moderated Activities become a defined stop in the overall visitor journey — checking off a physical challenge unlocks the next step, reward, or content in the broader experience arc.

items.title: Shared Gallery / Media Output
items.description: Photos captured by moderators feed into a shared event gallery or personal digital souvenir — giving visitors a visual memory of their physical achievement.

items.title: Crowd Games
items.description: Combine physical station results with digital crowd play — moderator-logged scores can compete alongside real-time game results in a unified event leaderboard.

items.title: Feedback & Survey Module
items.description: Follow a physical activity with an instant digital feedback prompt — capturing how the visitor felt about the challenge while the adrenaline is still fresh.

items.title: Live Hosting & Expert Staff
items.description: Trained moderators or activity hosts provided as a staffing service — briefed on both the physical game and the tablet logging flow, ensuring a smooth and energetic station experience.

items.title: Technical Setup & Live Support
items.description: On-site technical assistance covering tablet configuration, network setup across activity zones, and real-time troubleshooting during the event.

items.title: Custom Content Creation
items.description: Branded photo overlays, digital souvenir templates, or activity-specific visual assets that make every captured moment feel polished and on-brand.

items.title: World record wall
items.description: The top score at each physical station is enshrined as the event record — displayed live and updated in real time, giving every participant a reason to beat the current leader.

items.title: Team relay scoring
items.description: Individual scores at physical stations are pooled into a team total — groups compete across multiple activities, with the aggregate result determining the team leaderboard position.

items.title: Moderator photo booth moment
items.description: The moderator doubles as a photographer — staging a branded shot at the peak of the challenge, auto-framed with an event overlay and delivered to the visitor's personal recap.

items.title: Sponsor-owned activity station
items.description: A partner brand takes full ownership of one physical station — their name, their challenge, their reward — with all logged data forming a branded engagement report.

items.title: Physical + digital combo challenge
items.description: A physical activity result unlocks a follow-on digital challenge — the moderator's logged score becomes the starting condition for a screen-based game or puzzle the visitor then completes.

items.title: VIP challenge lane
items.description: A premium or backstage version of the activity — with a dedicated moderator, a more dramatic result display, and an exclusive digital souvenir — available to VIP token holders only.

items.title: Progressive unlocks across stations
items.description: Completing a physical activity unlocks the next one in a defined sequence — the moderator's confirmation is the gate, creating a structured adventure trail across the venue.

items.title: Live commentator integration
items.description: A live host or MC receives real-time result alerts from the moderator dashboard and calls out top scores, records broken, or dramatic moments over the PA — making physical results part of the event narrative.

items.title: Return challenge mechanic
items.description: Visitors can attempt the same physical station multiple times — the system logs their best result, tracks improvement, and rewards personal bests with a special badge or bonus score.

items.title: Highland Games festival
items.description: Strength and skill challenges — caber toss, tug of war, hammer throw — moderated and logged into a clan leaderboard, with kilted visuals and a dramatic championship reveal.

items.title: Carnival fairground
items.description: Classic fair games — ring toss, tin can alley, ball throw — reimagined with a digital score card, a prize tier system, and a personal recap delivered at the exit booth.

items.title: Sports combine & scouting day
items.description: Athletic stations measuring speed, strength, and agility — moderated by coaches with tablets — feeding a live performance dashboard that every participant can view and share.

items.title: Brand challenge circuit
items.description: A series of physical tasks built around a brand's identity — each station named after a product, value, or campaign — with scores feeding a branded leaderboard and winner recognition moment.

items.title: Science experiment lab
items.description: In a museum or education context, moderators log the outcomes of physical experiments — reaction times, building challenges, or measurement tasks — adding them to a shared discovery record.

items.title: Team away-day Olympics
items.description: Office teams compete across a series of absurd physical challenges — scored by a neutral moderator — with team totals feeding a live leaderboard and a trophy ceremony at the end.

items.title: Adventure trail quest
items.description: Visitors navigate a series of outdoor physical checkpoints — each moderated and stamped into their digital passport — completing the quest for a final reward and a full journey recap.

items.title: Christmas market challenge
items.description: Festive physical games — snowball throw, elf speed-stack, gift wrapping race — moderated and scored into a seasonal leaderboard, with a top-scorer gift reveal at the end of each day.
